Program_AdminRequired_Text:
This installer requires administrator privileges.

Please right-click and select 'Run as administrator'.
Program_GameRunning_Text:
Please close Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic first.

The installer cannot modify game files while the game is running.
Program_UninstallError_Text:
Could not determine the KOTOR installation path.

Please run the installer normally to reinstall.
Welcome_Description:
This installs the KOTOR Accessibility mod for Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (Steam).

The mod lets blind and visually impaired players play KOTOR with the NVDA screen reader.
Welcome_GameDescription:
The mod patches your Steam or GoG copy of Knights of the Old Republic.

If you don't own KOTOR yet, you can buy it from Steam or GoG. Otherwise click Next to choose what gets installed.
ModdingInfo_Description:
The following components are always installed alongside the accessibility mod. They are required for basic functionality and cannot be turned off.

Use Tab or arrow keys to read each line. Press Next to continue, or Back to cancel.
ModdingInfo_AccessibilityBody: Screen-reader output, keyboard navigation, audio cues. This project.
ModdingInfo_PrismBody: Routes spoken text to the active screen reader. Replaces the older Tolk library. The accessibility mod cannot speak without it. Credit: the Prism project.
ModdingInfo_WidescreenBody: Required for stable performance on modern monitors. Without them KOTOR has hardcoded 4:3 menus, alt-tab lag, and other display issues. Credit: Wogg (UniWS), ndix UR (KOTOR High Resolution Menus).
ModdingInfo_IniTweaksBody: Community-recommended graphics settings written to swkotor.ini: V-Sync on (fixes the 'character stuck after combat' bug on 60 Hz monitors), Frame Buffer off (fixes the 'crash after character creation' issue), Vertex Buffer Objects disabled (stability tweak for some GPU drivers), and FullScreen off (windowed mode for screen-reader compatibility).
ModSelection_Description: These optional mods improve the KOTOR experience. All are recommended and default to enabled. Uncheck any you want to skip.
ModSelection_K1cpDescription: Highly recommended. Fixes hundreds of bugs, softlocks, and quest-breaking issues. Without it, some quests can become unrecoverable mid-playthrough.
ModSelection_CutContentCheckbox: Restored cut content (JDR + Party Conversations on Ebon Hawk)
ModSelection_CutContentDescription: Restores conversations BioWare cut late in QA. Uses your locale's existing text and voice — no English bleed-through.
ModSelection_CompanionsCheckbox: Companion balance + Swoop upgrades (Thematic Companions + Swoop Bike Upgrades)
ModSelection_CompanionsDescription: Still vanilla but improved: companion stats matched to their backstory, plus two cut swoop upgrade items restored.
ModSelection_FootnoteBody: Note on Italian and Spanish installs: K1CP only ships official translation patches for German, French, and Russian. Italian and Spanish installs receive K1CP in English — the bugfix text will appear in English even though the rest of the game stays localised.
Main_StatusInstall:
This installs the KOTOR Accessibility mod into your Steam copy of Knights of the Old Republic.
The mod enables blind players to play using NVDA or another screen reader.
Main_StatusUpdate: This updates the KOTOR Accessibility mod to the latest version.
Main_BrowseDialogDescription: Select the KOTOR installation folder (contains swkotor.exe)
Main_PathNotFound:
swkotor.exe was not found in the selected folder.
Please select the correct KOTOR installation folder.
Main_ReadmeCheckBox: Open the getting-started guide in the browser
ModInstall_K1cpDownloading: Downloading KOTOR 1 Community Patch from GitHub...
ModInstall_K1cpApplying: Applying K1CP via HoloPatcher (this can take a minute)...
Main_VersionCheckFailed_Text:
Could not check for the latest mod version.

This may be because:
- No internet connection
- GitHub repository not found
- No releases published yet

Do you want to try downloading the mod anyway?
Main_ModDownloadFailed_Format:
Could not download the mod: {0}

The installation cannot continue without the .kpatch file.

You can download it manually from:
{1}/releases
and re-run this installer.
Main_ApplyFailed_Format:
The patch could not be installed:

{0}

If the message above mentions a hash mismatch or 'does not support detected game version', your swkotor.exe is a variant we have not seen before. Supported variants in v0.1 are Steam 1.0.3 and GoG 1.0.3 (including byte-equivalent CD re-packs). The Aspyr mobile / macOS port and pre-modified executables (UniWS, HR Menus) are not supported in v0.1.

Please file an issue at https://github.com/JeanStiletto/kotor-blind-accessibility/issues with the displayed hash and we will add your variant if its bytes match.

If the failure happened during the patch step itself rather than the version check, your swkotor.exe may need to be restored. Use Steam's 'Verify integrity of game files' (right-click KOTOR → Properties → Installed Files) or reinstall from GoG.
Main_CompleteModInstalled: The accessibility mod is installed and ready. Launch KOTOR and press Numpad-1 in the main menu to check that speech is working.
Main_CompleteModUpdated: The accessibility mod has been updated successfully.
Program_UpToDate_Format:
KOTOR Accessibility v{0} is already installed and up to date.

Would you like to perform a full reinstall?
InstalledOptions_Body_Format:
KOTOR Accessibility v{0} is installed and up to date.

Spatial audio (dsoal): {1}

Choose an action:
- Reinstall: re-runs the full installer to refresh all files.
- Toggle spatial audio: switch between modified (HRTF + EAX) and vanilla audio. Restart the game after toggling.
- Collect logs for beta test: build a zip in your Downloads folder containing the newest patch log and crash dump, ready to send to the developer.
- Close: do nothing and exit.
SpatialAudio_EnabledMessage:
Spatial audio is now ENABLED.

The dsoal DirectSound wrapper and OpenAL Soft renderer have been placed in the game folder, and EAX has been turned on in swkotor.ini. Per-source HRTF spatialization and EAX environmental reverb are now active.

Please restart KOTOR for the change to take effect.

Note: 2D streams (music, dialogue) may sound altered. If unacceptable, re-run this installer and choose 'Disable spatial audio'.

Licenses: dsoal and OpenAL Soft are LGPL 2.1. Source: https://github.com/kcat/dsoal and https://github.com/kcat/openal-soft
SpatialAudio_DisabledMessage:
Spatial audio is now DISABLED.

The dsoal files have been removed from the game folder and EAX has been turned off in swkotor.ini. The game will use Windows' native DirectSound on next launch.

Please restart KOTOR for the change to take effect.
Uninstall_Description: This removes the accessibility mod's own files only. K1CP and any other optional mods you installed alongside the mod stay in place. To return to fully vanilla KOTOR, verify game files via Steam or reinstall from GoG after this uninstall finishes.
Uninstall_Confirm_Text:
Are you sure you want to uninstall KOTOR Accessibility?

This will:
- Remove KotorPatcher.dll, sqlite3.dll, addresses.db, patch_config.toml, dinput8.dll
- Remove the patches\ folder (accessibility.dll + prism.dll)
- Remove the Add/Remove Programs entry

What is NOT touched:
- K1CP and any other optional mods you chose at install time stay in place
- Your save games are not modified
- swkotor.exe stays patched. To get back to vanilla, run Steam's 'Verify integrity of game files' or reinstall from GoG. That will also remove K1CP if you want a clean slate.
Common_CancelConfirm_Text: Are you sure you want to cancel the installation?
Logger_SaveLog_Errors:
There were warnings or errors during the operation.

Would you like to save a log file to your Desktop for troubleshooting?
Logger_SaveLog_Normal: Would you like to save a log file to your Desktop?
